name: Switzerland
contextBlurb: Swiss retail users can legally hold and use stablecoins. FINMA Guidance 06/2024 clarifies when fiat-referenced tokens can be offered without a full banking license if investors' redemption claims are covered by an irrevocable Swiss bank guarantee and AML/KYC rules apply. The SNB has no retail CBDC; it focuses on wholesale CBDC pilots on SDX.

legislation.title: Swiss Bankers Association releases a stablecoin expert report urging a pragmatic CHF-stablecoin framework.
BX Digital becomes the first FINMA-authorized DLT trading facility, enabling regulated secondary markets for tokenized assets.
FINMA Guidance 06/2024 published (licensing, bank-guarantee, AML expectations).

keyFigures.name: FINMA
keyFigures.role: Supervises banks and financial institutions, issues guidance for stablecoin structures, enforces AMLA, and assesses bank

keyFigures.name: Swiss National Bank (SNB)
keyFigures.role: Oversees financial stability and payment systems; runs wholesale CBDC pilots on SDX; no retail CBDC.

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Swiss retail users can legally hold and use stablecoins. FINMA Guidance 06/2024 clarifies when fiat-referenced tokens can be offered without a full banking license if investors' redemption claims are covered by an irrevocable Swiss bank guarantee and AML/KYC rules apply. The SNB has no retail CBDC; it focuses on wholesale CBDC pilots on SDX.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Function-dependent. A stablecoin may constitute deposit-taking/bank business (requiring a license) unless structured with a compliant bank guarantee. Some models may fall under collective investment schemes or securities rules. Not legal tender; AMLA applies.

regulators.name: FINMA
regulators.role: Supervises banks and financial institutions, issues guidance for stablecoin structures, enforces AMLA, and assesses bank-guarantee models.

regulators.name: Swiss National Bank
regulators.role: Oversees financial stability and payment systems; runs wholesale CBDC pilots on SDX; no retail CBDC.
